prompt:
A small photoresistor (LDR) with a round, light-sensitive top and two metal leads lying on a wooden desk next to a breadboard. The serpentine pattern on the sensor face is clearly visible, and in the background a simple circuit shows another LDR wired to an Arduino with a few jumper wires, suggesting that it is used to measure brightness. The focus stays on the single photoresistor in the foreground so its distinctive shape is obvious.

Near-future solarpunk electronics aesthetic with warm lighting, tidy tools, and a few plants or a tiny solar panel nearby. Photorealistic 512×512 rendering, no brand logos or legible text. The composition should clearly present the photoresistor as a light-dependent resistor inventory item.
